root_cause: CoreDNS configuration error is breaking cluster DNS
evidence: Pod payments/checkout-api-5dfc66f6f9-t8l2m has DNS failures while CoreDNS reports configuration errors
Representative workload DNS failure: DNS lookup failed for auth.payments.svc.cluster.local: lookup auth.payments.svc.cluster.local on 10.96.0.10:53: server misbehaving
Observed 3 DNS failure occurrence(s) for the current Pod within 20 minutes
Observed 5 DNS failure occurrence(s) across affected workload Pod(s) within 20 minutes
Representative CoreDNS config error: Readiness probe failed: Corefile:5 - Error during parsing: Unknown directive 'kubernets'
Observed 2 CoreDNS config error occurrence(s) within 20 minutes
CoreDNS Corefile forwards DNS back to CoreDNS itself
CoreDNS config and workload DNS failure signals persisted for 7.0 minutes

causes.message: Workloads depend on CoreDNS for cluster service discovery
CoreDNS is loading an invalid or recursively broken Corefile
Pod-local DNS lookups fail because CoreDNS cannot serve the configured zone correctly

pod:checkout-api-5dfc66f6f9-t8l2m: DNS lookup failed for auth.payments.svc.cluster.local: lookup auth.payments.svc.cluster.local on 10.96.0.10:53: server misbehaving
configmap:coredns: CoreDNS Corefile forwards DNS back to CoreDNS itself
timeline:coredns: Readiness probe failed: Corefile:5 - Error during parsing: Unknown directive 'kubernets'

likely_causes: The kube-system/coredns ConfigMap contains an invalid Corefile directive or syntax error
CoreDNS is forwarding recursively to itself or through a node resolver that points back to kube-dns
A recent CoreDNS ConfigMap rollout introduced an invalid plugin stanza or zone block
CoreDNS reload failed, so all Pods see generic DNS lookup failures while the root cause is shared config
suggested_checks: kubectl -n kube-system get configmap coredns -o yaml
Validate the Corefile syntax and plugin names before rolling CoreDNS
